Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 14:02 ET
Dispersion trade signals fire as institutions pile into single-name calls while hedging indices
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The 0.63 P/C split (index puts 1.48 vs single-name calls 0.85) confirms institutional dispersion positioning ahead of CPI.
  • 2. Negative gamma amplifies risk around Tuesday's CPI print with SPY dealers short $6.8B in hedging flow.
  • 3. COIN's massive call blocks and MRVL's technical breakout offer clearest risk/reward into earnings season.
NARRATIVE — dispersion setup pre-CPI

The most striking signal today is the textbook dispersion setup forming ahead of CPI, with institutions buying single-name calls while aggressively hedging index exposure. The 0.63 point spread between index P/C (1.48) and single-name P/C (0.85) represents the widest divergence since the August unwind, confirming smart money expects individual stocks to outperform indices. This positioning makes sense given implied correlation at just 0.20 — the lowest stock-specific regime reading in months. Dealer positioning amplifies all directional moves into Tuesday's inflation data, with SPY net gamma at $-6.8B negative — meaning any CPI surprise gets magnified by forced dealer hedging. The massive SPY put blocks (451K at $657 strike, 376K at $656) suggest institutions are pricing in downside protection around current levels, while the gamma flip sits just $7 above spot at $663. COIN's dual call blocks (12K contracts each at Apr $170 and Jun $170 strikes) totaling $48M in premium reflects conviction positioning ahead of crypto regulatory clarity, while the technical setup in MRVL (score 87.2 with golden cross confirmation) aligns with the 93% surge in call flow. The vol surface confirms this dispersion thesis, with 5 names showing backwardation (MRVL, TSLA, XLE, XLF, XLK) indicating elevated near-term event risk, while cheap vol/rich skew flags in NFLX and MU suggest single-name opportunities remain mispriced relative to index hedging costs.

Layer 1: Sentiment -18Layer 2: GEX -$6.8B negativeLayer 3: P/C split 0.63 divergenceLayer 4: IC 0.20 stock-specific
